content.claim: Official explanation attributes SEN growth 'partly' to modernization of diagnostic tools, but independent experts (clinical psychologist, teachers' union, 2025 reports) dispute that diagnostic modernization alone explains the magnitude; instead they attribute substantial causal weight to mandatory school enrollment from age six and stricter school-readiness assessments implemented in 2018, with acceleration post-2020.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Competing causal narratives (diagnostic improvement vs. policy-driven administrative change vs. real prevalence increase) have not been formally decomposed or modeled
claims about school-readiness rules and enrollment policy timing lack quantified impact estimates
COVID-era distance learning cited as compounding factor but not independently measured
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
